title: Smart Home Device Project
subtitle: Build a Real-World Automation Solution
overview: Students will design and build a smart home device using Arduino. They can choose from an Auto Fan, Smart Lock, Garden Monitor, or Parking Aid project. The session focuses on applying sensors, actuators, and programming to solve real-world problems.

images._note: Add photos of student projects, sensors, actuators, and final prototypes.

gallery.caption: Garden Monitor Using Soil Moisture Sensor
Students Presenting Their Smart Home Devices

youtubeVideos.title: Arduino Smart Home Projects
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how smart devices automate daily life.

youtubeVideos.title: Automatic Fan Arduino Project
youtubeVideos.desc: Build a fan that turns on automatically based on temperature.

youtubeVideos.title: Ultrasonic Parking Sensor Project
youtubeVideos.desc: Create a parking aid using distance measurement.

activity.title: Build Your Own Smart Home Device
steps: Choose a smart home project idea
Fix wiring or coding errors if needed
Explain how the project solves a real-life problem

projectOptions.name: Auto Fan
projectOptions.description: Fan automatically turns ON when temperature increases.

projectOptions.name: Smart Lock
projectOptions.description: Servo motor lock controlled by password or button.

projectOptions.name: Garden Monitor
projectOptions.description: Monitors soil moisture and alerts when watering is needed.

projectOptions.name: Parking Aid
projectOptions.description: Uses an ultrasonic sensor and buzzer to help parking.

quiz.q: What is a smart home device?
options: A device that uses automation and sensors
quiz.explanation: Smart devices use sensors and programming to automate tasks.

quiz.q: Which board is commonly used for smart home projects?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO is widely used for beginner automation projects.

quiz.q: What does a temperature sensor measure?
Which project can automatically cool a room?
quiz.explanation: The Auto Fan responds to temperature changes.

quiz.q: What component is commonly used in a smart lock?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors can open and close locks.

quiz.q: Which sensor is used in a garden monitor?
quiz.explanation: Soil moisture sensors measure water content in soil.

quiz.q: What does a parking aid measure?
Which sensor is commonly used in a parking aid?

options: Using technology to perform tasks automatically
quiz.explanation: Automation allows devices to work with minimal human effort.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of sensors?
What is the role of an actuator?
quiz.explanation: Actuators perform actions based on sensor input.

quiz.q: Why are smart home devices useful?
quiz.explanation: Smart devices save time, energy, and effort.

quiz.q: What should be done after building a project?
quiz.explanation: Testing ensures the project works correctly.

quiz.q: What skill is developed by creating smart home projects?
quiz.explanation: Students learn to solve real-world problems using technology.

quiz.q: What is the main objective of this session?
options: Build and demonstrate a smart home automation project
quiz.explanation: Students apply electronics and programming to create a useful smart device.
